steps.title: Identify the physical action
steps.content: The strongest clue is the setup: Mrs. Wilkins is “staying where she was” yet “reaching across to” a bowl of oranges. The phrase describes extending one's arm or body toward something without moving to a different location.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
“Stretching toward” expresses extending one's arm in the direction of something—the exact motion Mrs.

Wilkins makes toward the bowl. The other choices describe unrelated actions—association, catching up, or arrival at a destination—that don't fit this physical reaching.

That is the correct answer.
